metadata.summary: Build multi-agent vision pipelines: image-router, vision-extractor, validation-agent subagents; production VisionService with retry, multi-provider fallback, async batch, and image-hash caching.

content.title: Vision Agentic Pipeline: Production Architecture
content.summary: Production vision pipelines use a three-subagent chain: image-router (classifies image type, selects VLM + prompt), vision-extractor (calls VLM, enforces structured output, returns Pydantic model), validation-agent (cross-checks extracted fields against business rules). Low-confidence extractions (<0.85) route to a human-review queue. The VisionService class wraps this with retry logic, multi-provider fallback, and async batch processing.

why.p: A single monolithic vision call does not scale: different image types need different models (layout-heavy → Claude Sonnet; high-volume OCR → Gemini Flash; artistic/ambiguous → GPT-4o), and a single prompt cannot optimise for document extraction, classification, and moderation simultaneously. The router-extractor-validator pattern decouples these concerns, enables per-type model selection, and gates production writes behind business-rule validation.

VLMs cannot see file metadata (EXIF, PDF author, creation date) — agents expecting metadata from vision calls will get nothing; use PyMuPDF or Pillow for metadata extraction separately. Pydantic schema enforcement via prompt is unreliable without response_format/tool-use — always parse with try/except and fallback to a re-prompt with explicit error.

list.item: Agent-driven document processing workflows where images arrive as tool results from other agents.
Visual Q&A over a corpus of images (product catalogs, medical scans, satellite imagery).
Multi-provider resilience requirements where single-provider downtime must not block the pipeline.
High-volume batch document processing that must scale beyond single-threaded VLM calls.
Pipelines where business-rule validation on extracted data is required before downstream writes.
Simple one-off image descriptions — a single VLM call is sufficient; the router-extractor-validator overhead is not justified.
Real-time video frame analysis at >5 FPS — VLM API latency (500 ms–2 s) makes this impractical; use local CV models.
Privacy-sensitive images where sending to third-party APIs violates data agreements — use self-hosted Qwen3-VL or GLM-4.5V and run the pipeline locally.

section.name: recommended-subagents
section.p: Standard subagent decomposition for document processing pipelines:

list.item: image-router — classifies image type (document, photo, chart, medical) and selects VLM + prompt variant; runs upstream of all other agents
vision-extractor — calls VLM API, enforces structured output schema, returns Pydantic model; one extractor per document type
validation-agent — cross-checks extracted fields against business rules (date formats, numeric ranges, required fields, total sum verification)
alt-text-generator — generates accessible alt text descriptions; separate from data extraction to keep prompts focused

section.p: Chain: OCR agent → validation agent → structured-storage agent, with a human-review queue for low-confidence extractions (confidence < 0.85).
section.prompt:
Extract all data from this document image and return as JSON matching this schema: {schema}
Rules: - If a field is not visible or legible, set it to null - Do not infer or guess values not present in the image - For amounts, include currency symbol if visible - Confidence: set "low" if any field required significant interpretation

section.name: production-vision-service

rule.statement: For batch document processing, always use async clients with parallel execution (max_workers=5 to avoid rate limits). Single-threaded VLM calls at 1-2s each do not scale beyond ~50 documents/minute.
rule.rationale: A single VLM call takes 500 ms–2 s including network round-trip. At 5 parallel workers a pipeline processes ~150 documents/minute; at 1 worker it processes ~30/minute.

rule.statement: Cache extraction results keyed on image hash (SHA-256 of raw bytes). The same invoice scanned twice must not hit the API twice.
rule.rationale: Document scanning pipelines frequently encounter duplicates (rescans, retries). API-level deduplication via hash cache reduces cost by 10-30% in typical document processing pipelines.

rule.statement: Implement a multi-provider fallback: try primary provider; on exception, try secondary. Never surface provider failure to the caller as a hard error — exhaust all configured providers first.
rule.rationale: Provider outages are rare but guaranteed to occur. A fallback to a secondary provider (OpenAI → Claude → Gemini) maintains pipeline availability during partial outages without operator intervention.

section.p: Production VisionService with retry, fallback, and structured output:

section.name: multi-provider-fallback
async-batch-processing
gemini-multi-image
section.p: Gemini Vision API — single image and multi-image batch:
section.code:
import google.generativeai as genai from PIL import Image
genai.configure(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
def analyze_with_gemini(image_path: str, prompt: str) -> str: """Analyze image using Gemini Vision.""" model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-2.0-flash") image = Image.open(image_path) response = model.generate_content([prompt, image]) return response.text
def analyze_multiple_images(image_paths: list, prompt: str) -> str: """Analyze multiple images with Gemini (up to 3600 images).""" model = genai.GenerativeModel("gemini-2.0-flash") images = [Image.open(path) for path in image_paths] response = model.generate_content([prompt] + images) return response.text
section.note: Gemini 3,600-image limit sounds high but each image consumes ~250-750 tokens — real batch size is context-limited. Plan for ~500 images per request on average documents.

list.item: anthropic — Claude Vision API (pip install anthropic / https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/vision)
openai — GPT-4o Vision API (pip install openai / https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/vision)
google-generativeai — Gemini Vision API (pip install google-generativeai / https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-understanding)
pytesseract — local OCR fallback (Tesseract) for offline/air-gapped use (pip install pytesseract)
pdf2image — PDF to image conversion for document pipelines (pip install pdf2image)
Pillow — image preprocessing: resize, compress before API (pip install Pillow)
Anthropic API — best for complex document layouts; supports base64 and URL; agent-friendly
OpenAI API — best text rendering accuracy; structured JSON output via response_format; agent-friendly
Google AI Studio / Vertex AI — Gemini Flash for high-volume; Gemini Pro for medical/scientific; agent-friendly
Replicate — host Qwen3-VL and other open-source VLMs with simple API; pay-per-call
AWS Bedrock — Claude on enterprise-compliance infrastructure; stable API
Azure AI Vision — good OCR on Azure stack; less capable than frontier VLMs on layout
Roboflow — manages fine-tuned vision model datasets and inference endpoints

antipattern.description: Sending file metadata (EXIF, PDF author, creation date) expectations to the vision extraction agent.
antipattern.reason: VLMs cannot see file metadata — agents expecting metadata from vision calls will get nothing. Use PyMuPDF or Pillow for metadata extraction separately from the VLM call.

antipattern.description: Running VLM extraction synchronously in a web request handler for batch document uploads.
antipattern.reason: Single-threaded VLM calls at 1-2s each block the web worker. Move to async batch processing with a task queue (Celery, arq) — the response to the user is a job ID, not the extraction result.

checklist.check: Implement request queuing for high volume — do not block web workers on VLM calls.
Add caching keyed on image SHA-256 hash for repeated images.
Set up batch processing for bulk operations (async, max_workers=5).
Configure async processing for large files with progress callbacks.
Monitor response times and throughput; set SLA alerts at p95.
Implement circuit breaker pattern — stop calling a failing provider after N consecutive errors.
Add fallback provider for redundancy (primary → secondary → tertiary).
Set up health checks and monitoring with alerting for failures.
Image size limits: Anthropic API max 5MB per image, OpenAI 20MB — agents handling user uploads need pre-flight size checks.
For financial document extraction: require human review when confidence is "low" or extracted total exceeds a business threshold before writing to ERP.
Track token usage per request and monitor daily/monthly spend with budget alerts.
Compress images to 1024px long edge before API calls — reduces token cost 50-70%.
